name: BurstMate

consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem; requires it to be a CompItem. No layer selection is needed — the script creates all layers itself.

functions: guard against missing active composition and alert user
create 'BURST CTRL' guide shape layer with rect and stroke geometry
write StrokeBurst FFX binary to a temp file and apply it as a pseudo-effect preset onto BURST CTRL
write Void FFX binary to a temp file and apply it as a pseudo-effect preset onto BURST CTRL
set StrokeBurst effect parameters (widths, lengths, density, dash values, rotation offset) on BURST CTRL
create 'Stroke 1' shape layer with animated trim-path arc, repeater, and eased keyframes
create 'Stroke 2' shape layer with animated trim-path arc, repeater, and eased keyframes
parent Stroke 1 and Stroke 2 to BURST CTRL
wire AE expressions on BURST CTRL rect size, position, and stroke width
wire AE expressions on Stroke 1 path shape, stroke color, stroke width, dash/gap values, repeater copies, and repeater rotation
wire AE expressions on Stroke 2 path shape, stroke color, stroke width, dash/gap values, repeater copies, repeater rotation, and layer rotation
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'BurstMate'

scriptSim.scenario: success — burst layers created on active comp
scriptSim.inputs: Active composition 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 5s) containing one AV layer 'Background'; no layer selection required

app

expressions.target: BURST CTRL/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/1/ADBE Vector Rect Size

sim.note: Returns a constant [Width, Height] 2D vector from the Void pseudo-effect; sweeping time confirms static output

expressions.target: BURST CTRL/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/1/ADBE Vector Rect Position

sim.note: Returns a constant [Offset X, Offset Y] 2D vector from the Void pseudo-effect; sweeping time confirms static output

expressions.target: BURST CTRL/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/ADBE Vector Stroke Width

sim.note: Scales the property's own value by the inverse of a normalised unit-diagonal pixel length; produces a scale-invariant stroke width

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/1/ADBE Vector Shape

sim.note: Sweep Stroke 1 Length to observe how the stroke path endpoint moves radially outward from the burst center

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/ADBE Vector Stroke Color

sim.note: Passthrough color from StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Color' slider; sweeping the color channel luminance shows linear passthrough

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/ADBE Vector Stroke Width

sim.note: Direct passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Width' parameter; confirms linear 1:1 mapping

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Dash 1

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Dash 1'; confirms the dash segment length is directly controlled by the effect slider

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Gap 1

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Gap 1'; confirms the gap length is directly controlled by the effect slider

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Dash 2

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Dash 2'; second dash segment controlled by the effect slider

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Gap 2

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 1 Gap 2'; second gap controlled by the effect slider

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/3/ADBE Vector Repeater Copies

sim.note: Sweep Stroke 1 Density (integer copies for the repeater) to observe how burst spoke count changes

expressions.target: Stroke 1/ADBE Root Vectors Group/3/4/ADBE Vector Repeater Rotation

sim.note: Derived from Repeater copies count; produces even angular spacing (360/N degrees). Sweep copies count as the driving variable.

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/1/ADBE Vector Shape

sim.note: Sweep Stroke 2 Length to observe how the second stroke ring's path endpoint moves radially

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/ADBE Vector Stroke Color

sim.note: Passthrough color from StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Color'; sweeping confirms linear passthrough

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/ADBE Vector Stroke Width

sim.note: Direct passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Width'; confirms linear 1:1 mapping

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Dash 1

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Dash 1'

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Gap 1

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Gap 1'

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Dash 2

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Dash 2'

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/1/2/2/9/ADBE Vector Stroke Gap 2

sim.note: Passthrough of StrokeBurst 'Stroke 2 Gap 2'

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/3/ADBE Vector Repeater Copies

sim.note: Sweep Stroke 2 Density to observe second-ring spoke count

expressions.target: Stroke 2/ADBE Root Vectors Group/3/4/ADBE Vector Repeater Rotation

sim.note: Derived from Repeater copies count; produces even angular spacing (360/N degrees). Sweep copies count as the driving variable.

expressions.target: Stroke 2/Transform/Rotation

sim.note: Sweep Strokes Rotation Offset to observe how Stroke 2 rotates relative to Stroke 1; linear additive relationship

notes: HEADLESS script — no ScriptUI is built; the script runs entirely from the try/catch wrapper.

Effect parameters are configured via a pseudo-effect ('StrokeBurst', matchName 'Pseudo/stroke_burst') applied from an embedded FFX binary. The parameter internal IDs (e.g. Pseudo/stroke_burst-0008) are opaque; human-readable names are exposed in the AE Effect Controls panel.
A second pseudo-effect ('Void', matchName 'Pseudo/void') is applied to BURST CTRL to expose Width, Height, Offset X, and Offset Y as sliders that drive the rect geometry.
Stroke 1 and Stroke 2 are parented to BURST CTRL via setParentWithJump; their position/scale/rotation are inherited from BURST CTRL.
The 'Strokes Rotation Ofset' parameter name is intentionally misspelled in the script source (one 'f').
applyPreset() is AE-runtime-only; the fixture marks expected confidence as 'medium' for this reason.
All parameters support keyframe animation, but the script writes only two keyframe pairs (Trim Start/End) per stroke layer.
